# `policy`

The `policy` block configures how Terraform policy evaluates your policies, including which version of Terraform to use to perform the evaluation.

@include "beta.mdx"

## Configuration model

The `policy` block supports the following configuration:

- `policy` &nbsp block 
    - [`terraform_config`](#terraform_config) &nbsp block
        - [`required_version`](#required_version) &nbsp string  
    -  [`plugins`](#plugins) &nbsp block   
        - <plugin_name> &nbsp map  
            - `source` &nbsp string | required

## Complete configuration example

```hcl
policy {
  terraform_config {
    required_version = ">= 1.16.0"
  }
  plugins {
    example = {
      source = "../plugins/bin/example"
    }
    custom_validator = {
      source = "../plugins/bin/custom-validator"
    }
  }
}
```

## Specification

A `policy` block supports the following configuration. 

### `terraform_config`

The `terraform_config` block defines settings specific to the Terraform CLI provisioning the infrastructure. The `terraform_config` block takes the [`required_version`](#required_version) as an argument.  

- Data type: Block
- Default: None



### `required_version`

The `required_version` attribute declares the Terraform version required to provision infrastructure. Refer to [Version constraint syntax](/terraform/language/expressions/version-constraints#version-constraint-syntax) for more information.

- Data type: String
- Default: None
- Example: [Require minimum Terraform version](#require-minimum-terraform-version) 

### `plugins`

Specifies plugins that provide custom functions to use in your policies. Plugins must be compiled binaries. Refer to [Policy plugins](/terraform/policy/write/plugins) for more information. 

Specify each plugin as a named block within the `plugins` block.

- Data type: Block
- Default: None
- Example: [Configure multiple plugins](#configure-multiple-plugins)

### `source`

Specifies the relative path to a plugin binary in the same VCS repository as your policies. The binary must be a valid Terraform policy Go plugin server. Refer to [Policy plugins](/terraform/policy/write/plugins) for more information about creating and using policy plugins.

In your policies, reference your functions with the syntax `plugin::<plugin_name>::<function_name>(<function_arguments>...)`.

- Data type: String
- Default: None
- This argument is required to use the plugin.
- Example: [Configure multiple plugins](#configure-multiple-plugins)

## Examples

The following examples show how to write policy configurations for common use cases.

### Require minimum Terraform version

```hcl
policy {
  terraform_config {
    required_version = ">= 1.16.0"
  }
}
```

This example requires that the Terraform version used to evaluate these policies be greater than or equal to "1.16.0".

### Configure multiple plugins

```hcl
policy {
  plugins {
    example = {
      source = "../plugins/bin/example"
    }
    validator = {
      source = "../plugins/bin/validator"
    }
  }
}
```

This example configures two plugins that can be used in your policies with the `plugin::example::<function_name>` and `plugin::validator::<function_name>` syntax.

### Complete configuration

```hcl
policy {
  terraform_config {
    required_version = "~> 1.16"
  }
  plugins {
    custom = {
      source = "../plugins/bin/custom"
    }
  }
}
```

This example combines both Terraform version requirements and plugin configuration.
